element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Obtained from NPCs in towns; used in combat situations

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Central quest objective from narrative start
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Collected progressively throughout overworld and dungeons
Purchasing swords, magic, gathering story information
Traveling between dungeons and towns; encountering random enemies
Ring collection; boss encounters; environmental puzzles

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Oscillates between exploration/relief (towns, overworld traversal) and combat intensity (dungeons, boss encounters). Multiple gameplay style shifts break rhythm and provide tonal variety.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction in Wyclif village; basic sword combat against weak overworld enemies
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased enemy difficulty; introduction of dungeons and ring collection; magic spell availability
difficulty_curve.late_game: Boss fights escalate; side-on perspective combat requires different tactical approach; final confrontation with King Tsarkon
notable_spikes: First dungeon entry (shift from safe town to dangerous environment)
Boss encounters (sudden gameplay perspective shift to side-view)

notable_level_patterns.description: Towns serve as safe zones for equipment acquisition, NPC interaction, and story exposition. Top-down perspective maintained. Provides relief and resource management opportunities between dungeon runs.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Wyclif village (starting point); various merchant towns throughout realm

notable_level_patterns.description: Dungeons are structured around acquiring one or more rings of power. Contain enemy encounters, environmental challenges, and conclude with boss fights. Represent primary danger/tension zones.
Boss fights transition from top-down overworld/dungeon view to side-on arcade perspective. This mechanical shift signals escalation and narrative importance.
